qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsanitized provider rule labels allow arbitrary dynamic TOML injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets provider metadata templating and TOML decode in Docker/Marathon/ECS/Rancher dynamic configuration, while the advisory targets HTTP/3 TLS client-auth bypass in the TLS/QUIC stack during session resumption.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is a provider-rule TOML injection in Traefik's dynamic provider templating pipeline, which is distinct from the advisory's HTTP/3 TLS client-auth/session-resumption flaw.
evidence: The advisory summary is "Traefik affected by TLS ClientAuth Bypass on HTTP/3".

CVE-2025-68121 describes session-resumption acceptance in Go crypto/tls when ClientCAs or RootCAs change between the initial and resumed handshake, which is a TLS/authentication root cause rather than a configuration-templating issue.
The fixed Traefik release notes for v3.6.8 tie the advisory to HTTP/3/TLS maintenance, including an HTTP/3 quic-go bump in the same security release.

The only qualifying finding instead cites repo/provider/*, repo/templates/*.tmpl, and toml.Decode, showing arbitrary TOML injection through provider metadata rather than any HTTP/3, QUIC, TLS, or client-auth path.
